nameMeanings.name: Shaw
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): from Middle English s(c)hawe, s(c)haghe ‘small wood, grove, thicket’ (Old English sceaga). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a small wood, or habitational, for someone from any of the many places so named. Shaw and Shawe are most frequent in Lancashire and Yorkshire, where Shaw in Oldham (Lancashire) may be a principal source of the surname. The English and Lowland Scottish surname was also established in Ireland in the 17th century.
Scottish: shortened form of various surnames from the Gaelic personal name Sitheach, derived from sithech ‘wolf’.
Irish (Down and Antrim): adopted for Ó Síthigh ‘descendant of Sítheach’, a personal name based on sítheach ‘peaceful’. Compare Sheehy .

variants.name: Show
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cumberland): probably a variant of Shaw , perhaps representing a blend in Middle English of Old English sceaga and Old Norse skógr, both meaning ‘wood’.
Perhaps an Americanized form of German   Schau  .

variants.name: Sheehy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aileen, Aisling, Brigid, Declan, Egan, Eoin, Finbar, Ronan.

Irish (Limerick and Kerry): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Síthigh, ‘descendant of Sítheach’, or Mac Síthigh ‘son of Sítheach’, a form of the Irish personal name Síodhach ‘peaceful’, from the adjective sítheach ‘peaceful’ or ‘other worldly’ (see Sheehan ). The noun síth, from which this adjective derives, means basically ‘settlement’, hence both ‘peace’ and ‘place inhabited by other world beings’. This name was established in Ireland from the early 14th century, when a branch of the Scottish McDonnells, descended from Sítheach, great-grandson of Domhnaill, settled in the country.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Shaw was born in 1444, in Sauchie, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 daughter. She died in 1468, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 24.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Shaw est née en 1444, à Sauchie, Clackmannanshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1468, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 24 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Shaw wurde 1444 in Sauchie, Clackmannanshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1468 im Alter von 24 Jahren in London, Middlesex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Shaw è nata il nel 1444 nel Sauchie, Clackmannanshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1468, nel London, Middlesex, England, all'età di 24 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Shaw(은)는 1444년 영국 스코틀랜드 클랙먼아실르 소치에서 태어났다. 그녀는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1468년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 24세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Shaw родилась в 1444 г. в местности саучи, клаккманнаньшир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1468 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 24 лет.
